title: Self-signed certificate error in PostgreSQL connection
symptom: When attempting to connect to an AWS RDS PostgreSQL database, n8n throws 'self-signed certificate in certificate chain'. This occurs despite having custom CA certificates in place.
root_cause: The error occurs because the custom entrypoint script in the Docker container is overriding the default n8n entrypoint script, which is responsible for loading the custom certificates. As a result, the certificates are not being recognized, leading to the self-signed certificate error.
fix_steps: Modify the custom entrypoint script to include the logic from the default n8n entrypoint script that loads custom certificates.
Restart the n8n Docker container to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Update the custom entrypoint script to ensure it loads the custom certificates correctly.
